content: Migraine is a primary headache disorder affecting ~12% of population, more common in women (3:1). Characterized by recurrent moderate-severe headaches (4-72 hours), typically unilateral, pulsating, aggravated by activity, with nausea/vomiting and/or photophobia/phonophobia. Migraine with aura (25-30%): reversible focal neurological symptoms preceding headache, most commonly visual (scintillating scotoma, zigzag lines, fortification spectra), also sensory, speech, motor, brainstem, or retinal. Pathophysiology involves trigeminovascular system activation, cortical spreading depression (CSD) underlying aura, CGRP release causing vasodilation and neurogenic inflammation, and central sensitization. Triggers include stress, hormonal changes, sleep disruption, certain foods, weather changes. Chronic migraine: ≥15 headache days/month for >3 months, ≥8 with migraine features. Medication overuse headache develops from frequent acute medication use (>10-15 days/month depending on agent).
